assertion_results.text: Bug parsing correctly extracts all four required sections (Description, Steps to Reproduce, Expected Result, Actual Result) using the heading formats from bug-template-mock.md (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: bug-parsing.md contains all four required sections under '### Required Sections' with the statement 'All required sections are present in the bug description.' The sections are: 'Issue Description' (lines 36-40), 'Steps to Reproduce' (lines 44-49), 'Expected Result' (lines 52-55), and 'Actual Result' (lines 58-59). Each section includes substantive extracted content from the bug report.

assertion_results.text: Bug validation confirms the issue type ID (10020) matches the Bug issue type ID from Bug Configuration (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: bug-parsing.md line 13-15: 'Issue ACME-500 has issue type ID `10020`, which matches the configured Bug issue type ID. Proceeding with triage.' The Configuration Validated table also lists 'Bug issue type ID | 10020'.

assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies the trailing-whitespace heading extraction as the root cause — line[3:] does not strip whitespace (Steps 2-3)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md lines 16-24 trace the code path: 'section_name = line[3:]  # Extracts heading text after "## "' and explain 'When the heading line is `## Migration Patterns  \n` (with trailing whitespace), `line[3:]` extracts `"Migration Patterns  "` (with two trailing spaces). The extraction does NOT call `.strip()` on the result.' Line 69 summarizes: 'Single root cause: The heading extraction at `line[3:]` does not strip trailing whitespace, causing downstream exact-match lookups to fail silently.'

assertion_results.text: Root cause comment includes: what is broken (heading extraction does not strip trailing whitespace), where it is broken (plan-feature convention lookup), and how to verify the fix (reproducer test with whitespace in heading) (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: root-cause.md includes all three elements. WHAT: 'convention heading extraction uses `line[3:]` to extract the section name after `## `, but does not strip trailing whitespace from the result' (line 9). WHERE: 'plugins/sdlc-workflow/skills/plan-feature/SKILL.md -- convention heading extraction logic (`line[3:]` without `.strip()`)' and 'convention-aware task enrichment (exact-match lookup against unstripped keys)' (lines 13-14). HOW TO VERIFY: 'Create a test fixture `CONVENTIONS.md` with trailing whitespace on a heading line... Assert that the convention is still matched and included in the generated task's Implementation Notes despite the trailing whitespace. The test should fail before the fix... and pass after the fix' (lines 22-24).

assertion_results.text: Task description has reproducer test as the FIRST acceptance criterion — before any fix criteria (Step 5 Acceptance Criteria ordering)
assertion_results.evidence: task-description.md Acceptance Criteria section (lines 52-55). First criterion: '- [ ] A reproducer test demonstrates the bug: a `CONVENTIONS.md` with trailing whitespace on a heading causes the convention to be silently dropped (fails before fix, passes after fix)'. This precedes the fix criterion on line 53: '- [ ] Convention heading extraction strips trailing whitespace so that headings with trailing spaces are matched correctly'.

assertion_results.text: Task description has reproducer test as the FIRST test requirement — with explicit assertion guidance derived from the Steps to Reproduce (Step 5 Test Requirements ordering)
assertion_results.evidence: task-description.md Test Requirements section (lines 58-61). First requirement: '- [ ] Reproducer test: create a `CONVENTIONS.md` fixture with trailing whitespace on at least one heading (e.g., `## Migration Patterns  `), run convention extraction and matching, and assert the convention is included in the generated task output. This test must fail before the fix and pass after'. This includes explicit assertion guidance ('assert the convention is included in the generated task output') derived from the Steps to Reproduce (which describe running plan-feature and inspecting generated task output).

assertion_results.text: Task description includes a Bug Context section with Bug key (ACME-500), Steps to Reproduce, Expected/Actual Result, and Root Cause summary (Step 5 Bug Context extension)
assertion_results.evidence: task-description.md lines 66-71 contain a '## Bug Context' section with all required elements: Bug key '[ACME-500](https://mock-jira.example.com/browse/ACME-500)', Steps to Reproduce ('Create a `CONVENTIONS.md` with trailing whitespace on a heading...'), Expected Result ('The generated task's Implementation Notes should include the Migration Patterns convention reference'), Actual Result ('The generated task's Implementation Notes do NOT reference the Migration Patterns convention...'), and Root Cause summary ('Convention heading extraction uses `line[3:]` without `.strip()`...').

assertion_results.text: Task description sets Target Branch to main (Step 5 Target Branch)
Task description output includes ai-generated-jira in the Jira API metadata block showing it would be passed as a label to jira.create_issue (Step 5 Task creation)
assertion_results.evidence:
task-description.md lines 1-7 contain: '<!-- Jira API Metadata
jira.create_issue parameters:
  project: ACME
  issue_type: Task
  labels:
    - ai-generated-jira
-->'. The label 'ai-generated-jira' is explicitly listed under the labels parameter of jira.create_issue.
